title: Maintenance Plan
description: A reusable maintenance plan template. Defines what work to do (via maintenance_plan_items) on assets matching a scope. Equivalent to eq-solves-service's job_plans table. Plans can be tenant-global (customer_id + site_id both null), customer-scoped (customer_id set), or site-scoped (site_id set — legacy pattern).

external_id.description: External system reference (e.g. Maximo PM number, Delta JP id).

customer_id.description: When set with site_id null, plan applies to all sites of this customer.

site_id.description: Legacy / site-scoped plans. When null, plan is customer- or tenant-scoped.

code.description: Job plan code (e.g. 'LVACB', 'E1.25', 'M10.13'). Often combined with a frequency suffix in source documents — e.g. 'LVACB-A' parses to code 'LVACB' + frequency suffix 'A'. The suffix is stripped during import and the frequency is set on the maintenance_check, not on the plan.

name.description: Display name (e.g. 'LV ACB Service', 'Annual Switchboard PM').

description.description: Long-form description of what the plan covers.

frequency.description: Default frequency for instances of this plan. Often null when the plan covers multiple cycles (frequency lives per-item instead). Source codes use suffixes A/Q/M/S/W/2/3/5/8/10 — see x-eq-enum-aliases.

x-eq-cross-field-rules.message: A plan should be scoped to a customer OR a site, not both. (Both null = tenant-global, which is allowed.)

x-eq-import-hints.description: Plans are usually defined in a 'Job Plans' tab of a contract spreadsheet, or in a Maximo PM export. The plan code frequently encodes the frequency as a trailing suffix.

common_sources.name: Delta / Maximo PM export
watchouts: JP Code may include the frequency suffix ('LVACB-A') — split into code + frequency before insert
Frequency column may be a single letter (A/Q/M/S/W) or coded ('1Y', '6M') — see x-eq-enum-aliases

common_sources.name: Contract scope sheet
watchouts: Cost data belongs on contract_scope, not here
